rules.message: SQL embeds 2+ conditional-logic constructs (CASE WHEN branches / IF·IIF calls) — filtering/aggregation in SQL is fine, but conditional business rules here are hard to test and easy to duplicate; consider whether the policy belongs in app code. (Gated on two WHEN occurrences plus a SQL anchor keyword anywhere in the file, then flags the CASE line. The CASE line must be SQL-shaped — `CASE WHEN ...` or a bare `CASE` ending the line — so a TypeScript `case 'label':` switch clause never matches.) Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// query-logic-ok`.

await on a store/ORM call (receiver matching Repository$/Store$/prisma/db/orm/tx/trx) inside a loop or array-iteration callback (for/for-of/for-in/forEach/map/filter/reduce) — N+1 query pattern. Batch the fetch (e.g. findMany with an `in` filter) instead of one call per item. Restricted to BE handler paths (domains/*/routes/**, api/**). Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// n+1-ok`.

store.count()/prisma.<model>.count() called inside a loop or array-iteration callback — count() per entity (N calls). Use a single GROUP BY query or a denormalized counter column. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// count-in-loop-ok`.

A findMany()/raw prepare(...).all() result is reduced in application code (.reduce(...)) — if the row set is large, a SQL aggregate (SUM/COUNT/GROUP BY) avoids transferring it; if small and reused, app-side is fine. Decide by data volume. (Approximation: co-occurrence of a fetch-many call and .reduce( within the same function — does not verify the SAME variable is both the fetch result and the reduce receiver.) Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// app-agg-ok`.

A findMany()/raw prepare(...).all() result is counted in application code via .filter(...).length — if the row set is large, a SQL aggregate (SUM/COUNT/GROUP BY) avoids transferring it; if small and reused, app-side is fine. Decide by data volume. (Approximation: co-occurrence of a fetch-many call, .filter(, and .length within the same function — does not verify the SAME variable is both the fetch result and the filter receiver.) Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// app-agg-filter-ok`.

A read (findOne/findById/findUnique) feeds a branch that then calls create/upsert/insert (get-or-create / toggle pattern) — TOCTOU race: under concurrent requests two callers can both pass the check and both write (duplicate row / unique-constraint violation). Make it atomic: wrap the read+write in a transaction ($transaction), or replace check-then-write with an upsert / a unique constraint guarded by try-catch. Vetoed when a try/catch or a `$transaction(`/`transaction(` wrapper appears anywhere in the same function body — a guard anywhere in the function counts, not specifically around the write call. Co-occurrence within the same function only (no per-variable receiver correlation), like every other method-scan rule in this pack. The finding is reported at the read call's line, not the write's. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// toctou-ok`.
